title: aster proximus
intro: Aster proximus, also known as the Wood's Aster, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family, characterized by its purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Aster proximus include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of digestive issues, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Aster proximus can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and leaves in a tincture or salve.
